rulings.Case Title: FILAMENT LIGHTING, LLC VS. ARCHIPELAGO LIGHTING, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Archipelago Lighting Tec, Inc. As Successor Entity Judgment Debtor; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Scott R. Albrecht
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 19, 2018, Line 3, PETITIONER FILAMENT LIGHTING, LLC's Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Archipelago Lighting Tec, Inc. As Successor Entity Judgment Debtor; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Scott R. Albrecht Plaintiff Filament Lighting, LLC's motion to amend judgment to add Archipelago Lighting Tec, Inc. as a judgment debtor is GRANTED. Archipelago Tec is a successor corporation or mere continuation of judgment debtor Archipelago Lighting, Inc. (See Wolf Metals, Inc. v. Rand Pacific Sales, Inc. (2016) 4 Cal.App.5th 698, 705.) Archipelago Tec's creator, Jian Hua Ma, first denied creating any new entity after entry of plaintiff's judgment, but changed her testimony when confronted with plaintiff's knowledge of Archipelago Tec. (Ma Exam. 154-56.) Ma further admitted that Tec is a lighting company like debtor Archipelago Lighting. (Id. at 157-58, 160.) The two Archipelago entities have the same address (Albrecht Dec. Exs. H&L) and similar familial ownership. Ma stated that Tec had yet to be active. (Ma Exam. 164.) However, shortly after that assertion, Tec received a large check from a "Precision" entity, allegedly for goods. Archipelago Lighting formerly conducted business with a "Precision" entity; I thus infer that the check paid an account receivable. No due process issue exists, as Tec had notice of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Filament is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: DONNIE CUMMINGS VS. CHEVRON CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 19, 2018, Line 4, PETITIONER DONNIE CUMMINGS' Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award Donnie Cummings' petition to confirm the arbitration award is GRANTED. Federal Arbitration Act provisions regarding judicial review of arbitration awards do not apply in our California courts absent an express statement to that effect in the arbitration agreement. (Valencia v. Smyth (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 153, 174.) Here, the arbitration agreement's reference to JAMS Rule 25 does not call for FAA review of an award, because that rule refers to the FAA or applicable state law, and no preference for federal law is indicated. (See Mave Enterprises, Inc. v. Travelers Indemnity Co. (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1408, 1427-30.) The arbitration agreement says "arbitration shall be conducted in accordance with...the requirements of California law and the Federal Arbitration Act," but this refers to how the arbitration itself is conducted, not to how post-arbitration proceedings such as judicial review of an award are to be performed. There is no express indication in the agreement that the parties opted for FAA procedures for judicial review of an award, so California law applies. (Valencia, 185 Cal.App.4th at 174.) California has strong public policies favoring arbitration and disfavoring vacating an arbitration award absent quite limited circumstances. Ignoring these strong policies, Chevron's arguments for vacating the arbitration award focus almost exclusively on the FAA rather than on governing California law. Under California law, none of the situations that could justify vacating an award exist; e.g., no corruption or misconduct is alleged and the award bears a rational relationship to the underlying issues. (See Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. v. Intel Corp. (1994) 9 Cal.4th 362, 367.) Because a prevailing party in arbitration is "entitled to prejudgment interest as of the date of the final award to entry of judgment," prejudgment interest must be calculated from June 8, 2018 - the date of the final award. (See Tenzera, Inc. v. Osterman (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 16, 21.) Applying the Civil Code §3287(c) seven percent interest rate to the $511,533.95 final award for 164 days (June 8 to November 19, 2018), prejudgment interest is $16,088.79. The arbitration award is therefore confirmed in the amount of $527,622.74. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Cummings is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: HAR KWAN LUK DBA NANCY WONG, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS ET AL VS. TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 19, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT DAVID O'TOOLE, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default Defendant David O'Toole's motion to set aside default and default judgment is GRANTED. O'Toole has satisfied the three-part Stiles test and thus is entitled to equitable relief on the ground of extrinsic mistake. (See Stiles v. Wallis (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 1143, 1147-48.) (1) O'Toole's verified proposed answer establishes a "meritorious case" under Stiles. (Id.) (2) O'Toole has satisfactory excuses for failing to defend against the lawsuit: his confusion regarding summons papers due to five different lawsuits involving him and Nancy Wong; O'Toole's emotional and mental state; and the fact that an insurer's lawyer and a separate lawyer represented O'Toole, causing confusion over who would file his answer. (3) O'Toole acted diligently to seek a remedy upon discovery of the default judgment (mailing of notice of default is not dispositive of the time of discovery). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. O'Toole is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN (BATROA) ET AL VS. DIMITRIOU &ASSOCIATES, P.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kevin Sullivan'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (Ccp Sec. 430.10)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 19, 2018, Line 14, Defendant Kevin Sullivan'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (Ccp Sec. 430.10) Defendant Kevin Sullivan's demurrer to the first amended complaint is OVERRULED. "[I]n testing a pleading against a demurrer the facts alleged in the pleading are deemed to be true however improbable they may be." (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.) Yet Sullivan's demurrer would have the Court decide factual disputes in his favor. Even were the facts at issue at the pleading stage, I could take notice of a July 2016 pleading in which Sullivan represented to a court that he was "Attorney for Plaintiffs." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GILBERT PAPAZIAN II VS. KEVIN MICHAEL SULLIVAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admissions, To Have Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 19, 2018, LINE 4 DEFENDANTS GRIFFIN & SULLIVAN and KEVIN SULLIVAN'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Admissions, To Have Admissions Deemed Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Judge Pro Tem will email parties directly with tentative ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = BJH

rulings.Case Title: OMAR SHAFIE VS. MEGHAN MCGARRY DANGANAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Jobe Danganan And Meghan Mcgarry'S Motion To Quash Plaintiff'S Deposition Subpoena To Ucsf Health Information Management And To Grant A Protective Order And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 19, 2018, Line 8, Defendants Jobe Danganan And Meghan Mcgarry'S Motion To Quash Plaintiff'S Deposition Subpoena To Ucsf Health Information Management And To Grant A Protective Order And Sanctions Defendants' motion to quash plaintiff's subpoena to UCSF is GRANTED. This subpoena to a third party is not reasonably calculated to lead to admissible evidence. Rather, as the judge pro tem aptly observed, the subpoena regards a side issue to a side issue. Defendants' vague and overbroad motion for protective order is DENIED. No sanctions are awarded to anyone. rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, NOVEMBER 19, 2018, LINE 2, Motion 5 DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Judge Pro Tem will email parties directly with tentative ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = BJH

rulings.Case Title: THE SALVATORE ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. TWIN DRAGONS THAI, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 19, 2018, Line 12, DEFENDANT MELISSA GUGNI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Melissa Gugni's demurrers to the first amended complaint are OVERRULED. Alter ego liability is adequately pled. (FAC 12.) Contrary to Gugni's view, Business & Professions Code §17200 is not limited to "false advertising, bad faith insurance practices and the like." Operating a commercial building without taking statutorily required fire safety measures is a business practice that may allow for §17200 liability, and that is adequately pled. (See FAC 15-16.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Gugni is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
